business.headline: India overtakes the world's biggest economy in construction growthThe country has been investing heavily in highways, railways, airports, ports, metro systems and logistics networks. These projects are not only creating jobs and boosting economic activity today but are also laying the foundation for future growth.

Is your CIBIL score below 730? RBI's new rules may make loans harder to getThe RBI's Expected Credit Loss rules from April 1, 2027 could make banks stricter with borrowers below a 730 CIBIL score. The shift may raise rates, collateral demands and rejection risks for applicants with thin or irregular credit histories.

8th Pay Commission: Will state government employees get salary hikes too?The 8th Central Pay Commission has been constituted by the Union government to review salaries, pensions, allowances and other service conditions of Central government employees and pensioners.

From PAN, TDS to tax regime: 7 Form 16 checks you can't miss before filing ITRFor many salaried employees, Form 16 is the starting point of ITR filing. But before you file your return, have you checked whether your PAN, TDS credits and tax regime details are actually correct?

Gold and silver are falling from their peaks. 3 things investors must do nowThe shine appears to be fading, at least for now. After soaring to all-time highs earlier this year, gold and silver prices have come under pressure, leaving investors asking a familiar question: is it time to buy more, hold on, or head for the exit?

No E30 at pumps yet, so why has India exempted higher ethanol petrol from tax?India has exempted E22, E25, E27 and E30 petrol blends from excise duty despite no retail rollout yet. The move sets up a policy framework for higher ethanol fuels as questions persist over vehicle compatibility and costs.

Infosys, HCL Tech, TCS fall up to 3%: Why are IT stocks falling today?The Nifty IT index fell 2.67% to 27,525.80, emerging as the worst-performing sector on Dalal Street as of 9:50 am. The decline also weighed heavily on the Sensex, where IT majors were among the biggest losers.

Sensex, Nifty fall amid West Asia tensions; IT stocks tumble over 2%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 263.34 points, or 0.36%, to 73,719.84 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 70.70 points, or 0.30%, to 23,144.25 as of 9:24 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators pointed to a weak start for domestic equities. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,069 at around 7:43 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open below Wednesday's closing level of 23,214.95.

OpenDoor lays off entire India team of 250 in AI-driven overhaulOpenDoor is winding down its India operations and moving nearly 250 roles to the United States. The shift to smaller AI-native teams has sparked fresh questions about AI's impact on offshoring.

India scraps excise duty on petrol with 22% to 30% ethanol blendIndia has exempted petrol containing 22% to 30% ethanol from excise duty. The move is aimed at encouraging higher ethanol use and reducing reliance on imported crude oil.

Who borrows the most in India today: Gen Z, millennials or older Indians?India's love affair with credit is growing. As digital lending apps and easy EMIs make borrowing more accessible than ever, one question stands out: who is borrowing the most? Is it young Gen Z, middle-aged millennials, or older Indians?

Sebi proposes new salary disclosure rules for asset managersIn a consultation paper released on Wednesday, Sebi suggested that AMCs should be allowed to disclose the combined remuneration paid to executive-level employees along with the total number of such employees.

Markets end marginally higher as FMCG, banks gains cushion profit bookingThe BSE Sensex rose 64.42 points, or 0.09%, to close at 73,983.18. However, the NSE Nifty50 slipped 27.15 points, or 0.12%, to settle at 23,214.95.

EU widens Russia sanctions net, India-based entities among 50 firms targetedThe European Commission has proposed a new Russia sanctions package that could restrict India-based companies. The move widens scrutiny of third-country trade networks, but it still needs unanimous EU approval.

Zepto moves closer to IPO launch: What investors should watchAccording to the updated filing, Zepto's IPO will consist of a fresh issue of shares worth Rs 8,010 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 113 million shares by existing investors.

No 8th Pay Commission yet, but a DA hike could be around the cornerFresh inflation data has raised the possibility of a Dearness Allowance (DA) increase from July 2026 for central government employees and pensioners. The likely move could offer interim relief while the 8th Pay Commission continues to examine wider salary and pension demands.

Do you own too many mutual funds? How overdiversification can hurt returnsAdding a new mutual fund every year feels like a smart move. But at what point does diversification turn into overdiversification and start hurting returns? Let's find out.
Do stock market returns really beat FDs after taxes and risk?Ace investor Shankar Sharma has questioned whether stock markets really outperform fixed deposits once taxes and volatility are counted.

Will AI reduce hiring? TCS Chairman explains what lies ahead for IT jobsSpeaking at TCS's 31st annual general meeting, N Chandrasekaran outlined a future where AI agents become an integral part of the workforce.

entertainment.headline: Lagaan cast then and now: Where are they 25 years later? As Lagaan turns 25, the film is set for a theatrical re-release and a reunion of the people who made it unforgettable. On that note, here's a look at what the villagers of Champaner are doing in 2026.

CM Vijay's Mersal to re-release in theatres as actor-politician's pre-birthday gift Director Atlee's Mersal will return to theatres in Chennai and Chengalpattu on June 19 ahead of Vijay's birthday. The re-release gives fans a big-screen celebration as Jana Nayagan's release remains unclear.

Main Vaapas Aaunga, Governor, Backrooms: The story of great summer box office clash Indian cinemas are set for a crowded June 12 with nine releases across genres and languages. The line-up is being watched as a key box office test for the summer theatrical business.

PM Modi mourns Bharathiraja, calls him towering figure of Tamil cinema in tribute Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to iconic Tamil filmmaker Bharathiraja a day after the veteran filmmaker died at 84. His message highlighted the director's lasting impact on Tamil cinema and his acclaimed portrayal of rural life.

Welcome to The Jungle trailer: Akshay in 'fake' jungle madness and wildest cast ever The trailer for Welcome to the Jungle is out. Here's everything you need to know about Akshay Kumar's comedy adventure, including its cast, story hints, and what the trailer reveals.

7 savage jokes Pranit More could've cracked instead of rewarding Rs 370 biryani guy Pranit More rewarded the viral Gurugram man who said he expected sex after buying a woman Rs 370 worth of biryani. The exchange drew backlash, largely because many felt the comedian could have read the room better and turned the joke on the man himself.

Netflix not looking at Kannada cinema now: Kannada actor Sanchith Sanjeev Kannada actor Sanchith Sanjeev, nephew of Kichcha Sudeep, spoke to India Today about his debut film Mango Pachcha, challenges in Kannada cinema, nepotism and filmmaking dreams. He also made some explosive claims about the OTT space in Kannada cinema.

Amid Peddi row, Madhoo recalls when eve-teasing passed for romance in films Amid the ongoing Peddi controversy over Janhvi Kapoor's portrayal, actor Madhoo reflects on how Phool Aur Kaante glorified eve-teasing and why such depictions would be criticised today.

Dancing together: Priyanka Chopra dedicates unseen pics to late father Ashok Chopra Priyanka Chopra remembered her father Ashok Chopra on his death anniversary with unseen throwback pictures. She reflected on their timeless bond in a heartfelt message.

At 17, Pawan Kalyan almost joined Naxalites. Chiranjeevi's questions stopped him Pawan Kalyan said he had once considered joining the Naxalites before Chiranjeevi intervened. He said his brother's questions about family responsibility made him stop and rethink his decision.

Actor Isha Rikhi confirms marriage to Badshah, teases him over fan queries Isha Rikhi confirmed on Instagram that she is married to rapper Badshah. Her replies also addressed fan questions about their silence, missing posts and social media disconnect.

Backrooms review: Kane Parsons builds a fascinating maze not everyone will navigateRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Backrooms movie review: Kane Parsons' feature adaptation follows a troubled shop owner into the eerie Backrooms. The film impresses with its atmosphere and visuals, while its ambiguity may divide viewers.

Not Ranveer's fault: Ammy Virk defends actor in Don 3 row, flags Rs 45 crore demand Ammy Virk has backed Ranveer Singh in the dispute over his Don 3 exit. He questioned the timing of Excel Entertainment's reported compensation of Rs 45 crore.

Uorfi Javed exposes chilling rape and death threats after The Traitors win Uorfi Javed shared screenshots of trolling, rape threats and death threats after her The Traitors Season 1 win. Her post renewed attention on the abuse women routinely face on social media.

Why Bharathiraja returned his Padma Shri in 2009: Shall we break it or throw it? As tributes poured in after filmmaker Bharathiraja's death, attention returned to his 2009 protest over the Sri Lankan civil war, where he returned his Padma Shri award. The episode captured how his public activism stood alongside his long influence on Tamil cinema.

Taylor Swift's courtside appearance sparks buzz over who was seated behind her Taylor Swift's appearance at NBA Finals Game 4 came with an unexpected twist as she found herself just rows away from a familiar figure from her past. Here's what happened.

Daniel Radcliffe, Tom Felton spark Harry Potter nostalgia at Tony Awards after-party Daniel Radcliffe and Tom Felton reunited at a Tony Awards after-party at The Carlyle in New York, serving pure Harry Potter nostalgia.

David Harbour says false Millie Bobby Brown reports triggered mental breakdown David Harbour has said false reports about Millie Bobby Brown triggered a mental health breakdown last year. He said their disagreements were part of a family-like bond and that they still work together.

Obsession breaks horror box-office records, crosses $200 million globally Curry Barker's horror film Obsession has crossed $200 million worldwide to become Focus Features' highest-grossing release. Its run, aided by record holds and strong word of mouth, has also gathered momentum in India.

general news.headline: Live: Shatrughan Sinha denies joining rebel camp, reaffirms loyalty to TMCTMC Crisis Live Updates: As the political turmoil within the Trinamool Congress deepened, senior MP Shatrughan Sinha rejected reports linking his name to an alleged list of rebel MPs, asserting that he remains firmly loyal to Mamata Banerjee. Speculation is also growing over a possible merger of the Trinamool Congress with the Congress. Mamata Banerjee returned to West Bengal after concluding her three-day visit to Delhi, during which she met INDIA bloc leaders, including senior Congress leader Sonia Gandhi. Abhishek Banerjee remains in Delhi, with no clarity yet on when he will return to Kolkata, even as the West Bengal CID has summoned him in connection with the forged-signatures row. Stay tuned to India Today for the latest updates on the Trinamool Congress crisis.

Attacks must end: India condemns strike on vessel near Oman that killed 3 IndiansIndia strongly condemned the attack on a vessel off Oman that killed three Indians and called for an immediate end to attacks and a return to dialogue amid rising Middle East tensions. The development came after another tanker incident was reported off the Omani coast earlier in the day.
Baseless rumours: Congress rejects TMC merger buzz amid crisis in West BengalBaseless rumours: Congress rejects TMC merger buzz, says only national issues were discussed at meet

10 rounds in 5 seconds: Bishnoi gang shoots gym owner dead, warns of more attacksA gym operator was shot dead by motorcycle-borne attackers near Fawwara Chowk in Hansi during an early morning exercise session. A young woman was injured by bullet fragments as police and forensic teams began an investigation after CCTV footage surfaced.

The Great Millennial Career Crisis: I did everything right, but still felt stuckMillennials were told that education, hard work, and career loyalty would lead to success and stability. Yet many now face burnout, financial stress, career stagnation, and fears about AI. Through personal experiences and broader workplace trends, let's see why the traditional formula for success no longer feels certain for many.

IND A vs AFG A Live: Arshad Khan strikes to stem Afghan's strong startIndia A (IND) vs Afghanistan A (AFG) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: Afghanistan A (18/0 in 3 overs), chasing 294 in 38 overs. Anshuk Kamboj has been a little expensive at the start. He has opened the bowling along with left-arm pacer Arshad Khan. The second innings has been reduced to 38 overs due to rain.

Form human shield: Islamist Jamaat to march to India border over 'push-ins'Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and allied Islamist parties have announced that they would march to various border points with India on Friday protesting against the alleged Indian push-ins and killings near the frontier. The anti-Hasina student's NCP has called for Bangladeshis to form a human shield along the border even as high-level talks between India and Bangladesh are being held in New Delhi.

Choose us or Abhishek: TMC loyalist Kalyan Banerjee's ultimatum to MamataKalyan Banerjee alleged he was replaced as counsel at the last moment in the CID search operation case and accused Abhishek Banerjee of being "too arrogant." The veteran Trinamool MP also alleged that Abhishek Banerjee disrespected seniors, never trusted him, and was responsible for the party's deterioration.

Have you seen it? Kimi Antonelli trolls Kim Kardashian over his stolen towelFormula One: Kimi Antonelli's Monaco Grand Prix win shared the spotlight with a viral clip of Kim Kardashian using a towel laid out for him. The moment drew led to banter with the Mercedes driver joining in.

3 Indian sailors, missing after US strike on tanker off Oman, confirmed deadThree Indian sailors who went missing after a US military strike on the tanker Settebello in the Gulf of Oman have died, Union Shipping Minister Sarbananda Sonowal said.

How BJP got documents used to challenge Meenakshi Natarajan's nominationThe BJP said the documents used to challenge Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination were sourced from Telangana. Congress rejected the claim and said the BJP was trying to shift focus from its role in the election.

Priced at Rs 7.99 lakh, this 5-star SUV crossed 5 lakh sales milestonePriced from Rs 7.99 lakh (ex-showroom), the Hyundai Venue has crossed the 8 lakh domestic sales milestone in India, becoming one of the country's most successful compact SUVs since its 2019 debut.

Welcome to The Jungle trailer: Akshay in 'fake' jungle madness and wildest cast everThe trailer for Welcome to the Jungle is out. Here's everything you need to know about Akshay Kumar's comedy adventure, including its cast, story hints, and what the trailer reveals.

With ethanol blend uncertainty, waiting period for EVs stretches up to a monthAs uncertainty grows over India's future ethanol-blending roadmap, buyers are turning towards electric vehicles, resulting in waiting periods of up to a month for several popular EVs across segments.

Woman doctor issues video apology over male corpses' private parts commentThe woman, identified as Sejal Pawar, came under fire after a clip from Pranit More's show went viral on social media. Addressing the backlash, Pawar said she wanted to publicly acknowledge her mistake and apologise.

Why does POK have a separate president, PM and flag? It's a Pak conspiracyIf Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (POK) is controlled by Pakistan, why does it have a separate president, PM and flag? This is Pakistan's clever tactic, to fake autonomy while it keeps oppressing and killing Kashmiris in POK. Explained in details here.

Not just India, Diptyque's 'mosquito coil' is getting roasted in Philippines tooA French luxury brand has unveiled a scented spiral as part of its summer collection, and Indians can't stop laughing. The product's resemblance to a mosquito coil, coupled with its hefty price tag, has sparked a wave of amused reactions online.

I love inflation, says Trump. Do Trumpisms help us decipher POTUS?From declaring his love for inflation to inventing words like 'covfefe' and 'PANICAN', US President Donald Trump has spent years reshaping language to suit his political message, creating a catalogue of absurd Trumpisms that continue to bewilder critics and amuse supporters. Here are a few memorable ones.

Homemaker too small a word: SC says women running households are nation buildersThe Supreme Court said homemakers' domestic work and caregiving must be recognised in accident compensation cases. It said unpaid labour sustains families, shapes future generations and cannot be erased by marriage.

Elon Musk, population collapse in India and that nonsense called end of humanityElon Musk and other population collapse theorists exaggerate declining birth rates to stoke fears over demographic change and mask xenophobic anxieties about race, identity and immigration rather than any genuine threat to humanity's survival.

Why young Indians are risking fines and arrest to explore abandoned placesYou can now easily find Indian Surbex (urban exploration) content creators across social media platforms. The dramatic footage of deserted malls, crumbling factories and unfinished buildings, long-forgotten adventure parks often attract millions of views.

Third Indian ship incident reported in Oman in 3 days, last 2 were struck by USThe Indian Embassy in Oman said it is monitoring a vessel incident off Shinas port and coordinating with local authorities. The report came a day after three Indian seafarers were confirmed dead in another strike, intensifying concern over shipping safety in the Gulf.

Monsoon has stalled: What's blocking its vigorous march across India?After racing across large parts of southern and northeastern India, the southwest monsoon has entered a slower phase, raising questions about what comes next.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

sports.headline: Another Dravid in India colours: BCCI picks Rahul's son Anvay in U19 squad Rahul Dravid's younger son, Anvay, has been named in India's Under-19 one-day squad for Sri Lanka. The call-up rewards his domestic form and begins his pathway in the next World Cup cycle.

All you need to know about FIFA World Cup opening ceremony The FIFA World Cup opens with three ceremonies across Mexico City, Toronto and Los Angeles over two days. Shakira will headline the first opening ceremony in Mexico on Thursday, performing her official tournament single "Dai Dai" live for the first time.

ICC annual rankings update: India remain the world's top ODI side India retained the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC men's ODI team rankings following the annual update, although their advantage at the top was reduced. India now lead second-placed New Zealand by five rating points, while Australia remain third.

Have you seen it? Kimi Antonelli trolls Kim Kardashian over his stolen towel Formula One: Kimi Antonelli's Monaco Grand Prix win shared the spotlight with a viral clip of Kim Kardashian using a towel laid out for him. The moment drew led to banter with the Mercedes driver joining in.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group K preview: Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal in a pickle Group K offers a fascinating mix of established contenders and ambitious challengers. While Portugal and Colombia are expected to battle for top spot, DR Congo's resilience and Uzbekistan's defensive organisation could ensure every match carries significant weight in the race for qualification.

PV Sindhu's husband becomes an accidental coach. She has plenty to Sai about it Feeling slightly off, PV Sindhu asked husband Venkata Datta Sai to sit in her corner during her second-round match against Isharani Baruah in Sydney. She then beat her compatriot to reach the Australian Open quarter-finals on Thursday.

Australia add India-born Nikhil Chaudhary to T20Is squad for Bangladesh series Delhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary could become the first Indian-born man to represent Australia in international cricket in more than 60 years after earning a call-up to the country's T20I squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

IND A vs AFG A Live: Afghanistan off to strong start in 294-run chase India A (IND) vs Afghanistan A (AFG) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: Afghanistan A (18/0 in 3 overs), chasing 294 in 38 overs. Anshuk Kamboj has been a little expensive at the start. He has opened the bowling along with left-arm pacer Arshad Khan. The second innings has been reduced to 38 overs due to rain.

Harmanpreet Kaur vows fearless cricket as India prepare for Pakistan clash India captain Harmanpreet Kaur has vowed to play fearless cricket as her side gears up for a blockbuster start to its ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 campaign against arch-rivals Pakistan at Edgbaston in Birmingham on Sunday.

LA 2028 working to avoid repeat of World Cup visa issues in US: IOC chief Los Angeles Olympics organisers are working closely with U.S. authorities to ensure smooth entry procedures and avoid the visa complications that affected several stakeholders at the FIFA World Cup, IOC president Kirsty Coventry said on Wednesday.

FIFA World Cup arrives broken. Can football fix it again?The wait is over. Football's grandest stage is set once again. Yet before the spotlight turns to Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and a new generation eager to make its mark, the 2026 FIFA World Cup arrives under a cloud of scrutiny, carrying more off-field questions than any tournament would want on the eve of kick-off.

Chill, relax: FIFA chief shrugs off World Cup visa row in sharp exchange with reporterFIFA president Gianni Infantino said the organisation cannot dictate who the US government allows into the country for the World Cup, urging people to "chill and relax" over visa concerns.

FIFA World Cup 2026 by the numbers: Breaking down the largest edition everThe 2026 FIFA World Cup will open a 48-team tournament across the United States, Canada and Mexico on June 11. Its scale is unmatched, with 104 matches, 1,248 players and several records under threat.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

Ben Stokes made a mistake, but shouldn't be sacked as Test captain: Michael VaughanMichael Vaughan has defended Ben Stokes amid scrutiny over his curfew breach, arguing that while the England captain made a mistake, the incident does not justify removing him from the Test captaincy.

Making sure right people come to US: Trump on FIFA World Cup visa rowUS President Donald Trump said his administration is working to ensure that "the right people" are allowed into the country for the FIFA World Cup 2026 as the United States prepares to host the tournament.

Hitman in range: Rohit Sharma gets into groove for India vs Afghanistan ODIsRohit Sharma returned to India's white-ball nets and gradually found his range after a lay-off. The session eased immediate worries and showed a senior batter measuring himself against his own standards.

Explosive Richa Ghosh finds form, but India lose final T20 World Cup warm-upWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Richa Ghosh hit 68 off 36, but India lost to England in Cardiff in their final warm-up match. Her counterattack eased concerns before Sunday's World Cup opener, though India's top order still remains under scrutiny.

FIFA World Cup coverage in India headlined by Igor Stimac, Gurpreet SandhuWith the FIFA World Cup 2026 just days away, ZEE5 has assembled a star-studded multilingual commentary team featuring Bhaichung Bhutia, Gurpreet Singh Sandhu, Igor Stimac and I.M. Vijayan to bring the tournament closer to football fans across India.

After missing IPL, MS Dhoni promotes inaugural edition of Jharkhand T20 leagueMS Dhoni attended the opening ceremony of the inaugural Jharkhand T20 League in Ranchi on Wednesday. His presence gave the new tournament a high-profile start.

FIFA World Cup fever grips Kerala as God's own country prepares for tournamentWorld Cup fever has engulfed Kerala, with flags, cutouts and flex battles stretching across the state. Beneath the carnival sits a familiar truth: deep football culture, thin grassroots support and one more emotional wait for Messi, Ronaldo and Neymar.

Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson dropped from England Test squad after nightclub incidentEngland captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been dropped for the second Test against New Zealand after breaching a midnight curfew at a London nightclub. Stokes is reportedly considering his future as the Test captain of England.

Security breach sees Messi, Argentina's World Cup squad's passport details leakedAn official team sheet at Alabama's Jordan-Hare Stadium reportedly exposed the passport details of Lionel Messi and Argentina's entire squad before the Iceland friendly. The leak raised fresh concerns over player privacy and data security days before Argentina begin their World Cup defence.

Manchester United captain Fernandes, Keane bury hatchet after candid chatRoy Keane said he has spoken to Bruno Fernandes after a row over comments about the Premier League assist record between the duo. The exchange closed a misunderstanding that Fernandes said had distorted his original remarks.

Haiti will wear Poland's flag on their FIFA World Cup 2026 jersey. Here's whyHaiti are set to make their second appearance at the FIFA World Cup this year, but there seems to be a small tribute on the kit to Poland with the presence of their flag.

World Cup, Group J preview: Defenders Argentina prepare for Messi's farewellFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's Argentina prepare for one last dance for the legendary footballer. Argentina have been drawn against Algeria, Austria and Jordan in the Group stage.

Australian Badminton: Sindhu storms into 2nd round, Tanvi upsets 5th seedIndia's PV Sindhu and emerging star Tanvi Sharma marched into the second round of the Australian Badminton Open. Sindhu, having returned to Top 10 in singles rankings hammered Peru's Ines Castillo in straight games.

Priyank Panchal launches India's first athlete-led sports education hubSportsversity has launched an athlete-first sports education platform with Priyank Panchal as strategic adviser. The venture aims to open structured pathways into non-playing careers across India's expanding sports industry.

Shubman Gill becomes top-ranked Indian Test batter, Jasprit Bumrah retains top spotIndia captain Shubman Gill is now the country's top Test batter in the latest ICC rankings, while Jasprit Bumrah remains the No.1 Test bowler. Harry Brook climbs to the global summit.

Pakistan cricket to decide futures of captain Masood, Sarfaraz over weekendPakistan Cricket Board officials will meet this weekend to decide whether Shan Masood and Sarfaraz stay in charge after the 0-2 defeat in Bangladesh. The review could trigger a wider reset, with Salman Ali Agha, Younis Khan and governance changes all in play.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
